title: Exact Finite K-Means Clustering Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite k-means bad centroid QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact finite-cluster replay computes the first centroid x-coordinate as -1 while a malformed row claims -1/2, isolating a compact average-equation conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional clustering rows only when they add distinct assignment, Voronoi-boundary, objective-comparison, initialization, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep Lloyd convergence, global optimality, statistical guarantees, and floating-point implementations in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed rational point table, cluster assignments, centroids, residuals, squared distances, WCSS, global centroid, total squared deviation, and between-cluster decomposition as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false centroid-coordinate row through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting value.
Expose the pack through the probability/statistics, matrix computation, optimization, and numerical-analysis learner and consumer query surfaces.
Keep Lloyd convergence, k-means global optimality, clustering consistency, initialization guarantees, NP-hardness, and floating-point implementations marked Lean or numerical-honesty horizon.
